whatUsuallyDecidesTheOutcome: Existing operating model, contracts, policy tooling and FinOps maturity.
caution: Small score differences rarely justify changing the strategic cloud.

whatUsuallyDecidesTheOutcome: Workforce identity, SIEM ecosystem, regulatory evidence and security operations model.
caution: Avoid buying overlapping security tools without an integration plan.

whatUsuallyDecidesTheOutcome: Global footprint, hybrid circuits, private access, latency and egress.
caution: Network designs are workload-specific; region and topology can reverse a default.

whatUsuallyDecidesTheOutcome: Required control, runtime portability, team skill and operational burden.
caution: Do not default to Kubernetes or VMs when managed runtimes suffice.

whatUsuallyDecidesTheOutcome: Data model, consistency, latency, engine compatibility and data gravity.
caution: Database migrations are expensive; benchmark with representative workloads.

whatUsuallyDecidesTheOutcome: Data gravity, semantic layer, BI adoption, streaming and governance.
caution: Avoid fragmented lake, warehouse and BI platforms with duplicated ownership.

whatUsuallyDecidesTheOutcome: Model quality, data integration, evaluation, safety, agents and accelerator needs.
caution: Model and service capabilities change rapidly; revalidate before commitment.

whatUsuallyDecidesTheOutcome: Enterprise applications, API lifecycle, connector estate and user channels.
caution: Distinguish integration, eventing, workflow and API-management requirements.

whatUsuallyDecidesTheOutcome: Developer tooling, golden paths, observability standards and migration estate.
caution: Toolchain duplication creates cognitive load and control gaps.

whatUsuallyDecidesTheOutcome: Device, latency, disconnected, media or sovereign requirements.
caution: Some providers rely on partners; validate lifecycle and support ownership.
